title: Automating Rising and Falling Wedge Detection in MQL5
author: Christian Benjamin (lynnchris)
verdict: MEDIUM — Wedge = converging trendlines. Rising wedge: both slopes positive + lower slope > upper slope. Falling wedge: both negative + upper slope < lower slope. Convergence check (apex beyond current bar) prevents invalid patterns. Good for zone context classification.

wedge_detection.rising_wedge: upperSlope > 0 AND lowerSlope > 0 AND lowerSlope > upperSlope (lower boundary rising faster)
wedge_detection.falling_wedge: upperSlope < 0 AND lowerSlope < 0 AND upperSlope < lowerSlope (upper boundary falling faster)
wedge_detection.apex_validation: apexX must be AFTER current bar (apex in future = still converging, valid wedge)

PivotLeft.description: Bars left of pivot for confirmation

PivotRight.description: Bars right of pivot (delayed confirmation = anti-repainting)

BreakoutBuffer.description: Points*3 beyond line for breakout trigger

classification.rising_wedge: Bearish — price coiling up into resistance, expect downside break
classification.falling_wedge: Bullish — price coiling down into support, expect upside break
classification.breakout_direction: Rising wedge breaks DOWN; Falling wedge breaks UP

keep: Convergence check: apex must be beyond current bar
PivotRight=5 anti-repainting (wait for 5 right-side bars before confirming)
discard: MaxWedges limit (not applicable to our system)
improve: Wedge type in zone context: zone inside falling wedge = high-probability demand zone
Wedge + zone at convergence point = maximum compression entry
Use slope comparison to classify our M15 structure: converging trendlines = wedge market
